behaviors.description: Generated function signature matches expected interface from the natural-language spec.
behaviors.justification: This behavior evaluates whether the real model produces the correct parameter names/types/return type from text, which is model-output quality. A stub would hide this failure. Workflow is disqualified because this is a single generation decision, not a multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real LLM generation for a spec with strict signature requirements; parse the generated function signature and assert exact interface match.

behaviors.description: Orchestration runs attempts in order and exits on first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff/orchestration state (generator->tester->attempt controller), not model judgment quality. Deterministic stubs can reproduce ordering/early-return bugs. System is disqualified because no real OS/network/subprocess behavior is required; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because correctness does not depend on model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with stubbed generator and test runner results (fail, fail, pass) and assert attempt order, stop-at-pass behavior, and no extra calls after pass.

behaviors.description: Live iterative loop can reach a passing implementation within allowed attempts when one-shot usually fails.
behaviors.justification: This requires multiple real model decisions in sequence (generate->test feedback->refine->retest). Mocking the model would hide the key failure mode (whether iterative model behavior improves). Lower levels are disqualified by explicit 'live iterative loop' language.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end with real LLM on a target prompt known to often fail first attempt; verify a later attempt (<=3) passes the provided tests.

behaviors.description: On test failure, refinement prompt builder includes failed test names and failure messages.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is data handoff from test-execution results into prompt-construction modules. Deterministic fixtures catch omissions/mapping bugs. Agentic is disqualified because this behavior checks prompt payload construction, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed orchestrator/prompt-builder with synthetic failed test results and assert next prompt contains each failed test name and failure message.

behaviors.description: Next live attempt changes implementation in response to prior failure context, not resubmitting identical code.
behaviors.justification: Requires at least two real agentic steps and validates context-carryover effect on subsequent model output. A stubbed model would trivially pass/fail without testing adaptation. Agentic is considered but rejected because behavior explicitly depends on sequenced decisions across attempts.
behaviors.test_description: Run live generate->fail->refine cycle with real LLM; compare attempt 1 vs attempt 2 code and assert substantive change aligned with failure context.

behaviors.description: After 3 failed attempts, service returns structured error including all attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/bookkeeping/serialization across local modules. Deterministic failing stubs reproduce real failure modes (off-by-one attempts, missing attempt metadata). System is disqualified: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified: no dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use stub generator/tester that always fail; assert exactly 3 attempts executed and returned error contains all three attempt payloads with test outcomes.

behaviors.description: Full non-agentic pipeline wiring works end-to-end with deterministic stub model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to non-agentic end-to-end wiring of local modules. This is exactly integration-level validation of module composition. Higher levels are disqualified by explicit deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline with fixed stub model outputs and deterministic test runner responses; assert generate call, test execution, and attempt bookkeeping all connect correctly.

behaviors.description: Full live loop solves at least one known hard function that fails on first attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is a full generate->test->refine->retest loop with real model behavior and success criterion across multiple agentic steps. Integration is disqualified by 'full live loop'; Agentic-only is disqualified because correctness depends on sequenced multi-step adaptation, not one model call.
behaviors.test_description: Run live loop against a curated hard task where first attempt is expected to fail; verify later attempt within limit passes and final output satisfies the test suite.
